text_source: Urp...
Blurgh. I ate way too much. I feel like food's gonna\n
come out the wrong way if I open my mouth...
As I lie down rubbing my stomach, Kuon digs through\n
her bags, searching for something.
How can she still move around like that? She ate\n
more than double her own body weight...
She REALLY doesn't look like she eats as much as she\n
does. Where does it all go, with that figure?
Hmmhmm...
What are you doing?
She seems to be in a good mood, humming to herself\n
happily.
Eh heh heh. Getting ready to take a bath, of course.
I've just been wiping down for the past few days,\n
so as long as we have baths, I'm taking advantage.
Kuon has a dreamy look on her face...
A bath, huh? You can't get a proper one on the road,\n
that's for sure.
It's too bad we can't soak, but a bath's a bath.
Huh? It's a bath, but you can't soak in it?
Oh, they're steam baths! They're much more common\n
than baths with running water around here.
Ah, I see.
No hot tubs, then... I'd prefer a good soak, but a\n
steam bath still sounds pretty good right now.
You should go take one later, too. It'll melt away\n
the fatigue and cleanse you, body and soul.
Guess you're right...
Now that she mentions it, I did sweat a whole lot\n
exerting myself on the trek earlier...
Too bad about the hot water, but it'll feel nice to\n
relax in the steam.
All right, I'll go do that, then.
Uh.
Kuon makes a noise as if she's suddenly remembering\n
something.
Uh... hm. Hmm...
Then she puts a finger to her chin, deep in thought.
...Why is she looking at me like that?
I still have a few things I need to take care of,\n
actually.
Hm?
Yeah. It might take a while, so why don't you go\n
take a bath first?
Oh, I don't mind waiting.
My stomach's so full, I don't think I can move from\n
this spot anyway. Besides, I want to relax.
No, you're probably tired, right? Go ahead and take\n
it now. Here--a spare change of clothes for you.
She pushes a set of clothes into my hands. When did\n
she put those together?
You haven't forgotten how to take a bath, right?
I seriously doubt that. I mean, I HOPE I haven't.
Then you should be fine on your own. Don't worry\n
about me and go relax, OK?
If you insist...
I have a weird feeling about this, but it'd be rude\n
to refuse her goodwill, so...
The bath's downstairs and to the left, at the end\n
of the corridor, OK?
OK, got it.
As I leave the room and head left, a long hallway\n
greets me, as promised.
The door at the end of the hall opens into something\n
like a changing room, lined with shelves.
Doesn't seem like anyone else is using the bath\n
right now. I should be able to unwind without worry.
I untie my sash and toss my clothes haphazardly\n
onto a random shelf.
I open another door at the far end of the changing\n
room, and a blast of hot air washes over me...
The bath beyond is spacious for an inn like this.\n
Benches line the walls, enough to seat five people.
Set into the opposite wall is something like a\n
furnace without an opening, fenced in by wood.
Judging by the heat waves rising from it, you\n
probably pour water over that to make the steam...
Hoomph. Ah...
I flop onto one of the wooden benches and let out\n
a deep sigh.
The room's warmth suffuses me. I didn't realize how\n
good this would feel.
Ahhh...
I zone out, staring up at the ceiling and thinking.\n
The day's events wash over me...
I woke up in a strange place...
Then, a monstrous creature attacked me, only for\n
Kuon to save me in the nick of time.
And now, here I am with no memory, no belongings,\n
no power. No place to go home to. A lot of nothing.
What am I going to do...?
Bah, no point stressing over it. As it stands,\n
I can't do much but trust in Kuon's hospitality.
We'll see how things play out...
Taking a ladle from the bench, I throw water over\n
the heating apparatus.
The hiss of evaporating water and a cloud of steam\n
fill the room, heating the air.
Gah. I walked so much, my calves are all swollen.
The arches of my feet, too. Ow.
A dull pain shoots through my foot when I try to\n
rub the ache away...
Perturbed, I lift my foot, looking at the sole to\n
find the source of the pain...
Huh?
A loud thud interrupts me from the other side of\n
the wall.
What the... Did something collapse? Doesn't matter,\n
I guess.
I look back down at the poor sole of my foot--rife\n
with blisters, some of them torn and bloodied.
Yikes, the skin's peeling, too. I guess we did walk\n
a pretty long way...
Probably better not to touch that. Left with no\n
choice, I return to massaging my sore calves\n
instead.
...Huh?
Suddenly, I get the feeling I'm being watched.
I glance around the steam-filled room, but of\n
course, no one is in here with me...
...Probably just my imagination.
Oh, well. I should loosen up my other muscles while\n
I'm in here--it's not just my calves that hurt.
Up we go...
Careful of my feet, I ease myself up and stand\n
upright, stretching out my whole body.
*Flutter, fwumph*
*THUNK*
Oops, the washcloth around my waist is--wait, there's\n
that noise again. Where's that coming from?
*Squeeeaak. Squeak, squeak, chirp.*
...some kind of small animal? Who knows what weird\n
creatures the innkeeper might have around...
Well, no big deal. As long as it minds its own\n
business. Now, I'll start with some loosening up...
Shake-shake-shake-shake--
*Thwap, fwup, thwap--*
*WHOMP, CRASH, THUNK*
Gyeek!
That thing's making a lot of noise out there. What's\n
with the ruckus?
Whatever. Next, some hip swivels--
Hup! Hup, hmph, hah!
*Thwip, thwip, thwap--*
*THUNK*\n
*Rattle, rattle*...
All right, here we go! Pick up the pace!
*Jiggle, jiggle, thwip--*
*WHUMP* *clatter*
Fwaaaaaaaaah!!...
Something crashes loudly and scampers off...
Just animals fighting. I don't really know the\n
seasons here, but I guess spring must be close.
Loosened up, I sit back down on the bench and\n
surrender myself to the relaxing steam.
Ahhh, that hits the spot...
